title: Garr
content:
{{Top|leg}} {{Character |type= |image= |name=Garr |homeworld=[[Excarga/Legends|Excarga]]''[[Boba Fett: Crossfire]]'' |birth=[[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]][[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]] and Garr are both ten [[Standard year/Legends|years]] old in the book ''[[Boba Fett: Crossfire]]'' that ''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' dates to [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]]. Therefore, Garr's birth year can be calculated to [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]]. |death= |species=[[Excargan]]''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Genderless]] |pronouns=new |height= |mass= |hair= |feathers= |eyes=[[Color/Legends|Brown]] |skin=BrownGarr stated to have ''"darker skin"'' than [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]] in ''[[Boba Fett: Crossfire]]'', and to be ''"dark-skinned"'' in ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''. |cyber= |families=new |parents=new |partners=new |siblings=new |children=new |affiliation= |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|Did you ever wonder why I don't hang out with the other ten-year-olds? They want to treat you one way if you're a boy, and another way if you're a girl, and there's no in-between. No way to be just a kid, just a person.''"
"''Stupid. Can't they just treat you as a friend?''"
"''Nope. But come on! Let's find something to do!|Garr and Boba Fett|}} '''Garr''' was an [[Excargan]] whose [[Garr's mother|mother]] and [[Garr's father|father]] were presumed [[Death/Legends|dead]] after [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]] [[Battle of Excarga|invaded]] [[Excarga/Legends|Excarga]]. Along with other orphans, Garr traveled on the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Republic]] [[Starship/Legends|starship]] ''[[Candaserri]]'' to an orphanage on [[Bespin/Legends|Bespin]]. [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]], who came aboard shortly after the [[Conquest of Raxus Prime]], was the same age as Garr and they quickly became friends. They parted on Bespin when [[Aurra Sing/Legends|Aurra Sing]] caught up to Fett, and Garr sought help from the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Glynn-Beti]] and [[Ulu Ulix]] despite Fett's hatred of the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]]. ==Biography== ===Life before the ''Candaserri''=== Garr was born in [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]] on the mining planet [[Excarga/Legends|Excarga]] to a [[Garr's mother|mother]] and [[Garr's father|father]] who were [[Ore/Legends|ore]] traders. As a ten-[[Standard year/Legends|year]]-old [[Excargan]] in [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]], Garr's [[Gender/Legends|gender]] had not yet been determined; until Excargans experienced physical changes around age thirteen, they were genderless. When the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]] [[Battle of Excarga|invaded]] to take control of the mines, Garr's [[Parent/Legends|parents]] hid their child while they themselves were made [[Prisoner/Legends|prisoners]]. After a [[Battle of Excarga|counterattack]] by the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Republic]], Garr's parents could not be found and were presumed dead, although Garr believed they were alive and would return for their child. The orphan was taken to the Republic [[Starship/Legends|starship]] ''[[Candaserri]]'', which was journeying to an orphanage on [[Bespin/Legends|Bespin]] with other younger children. ===Friendship with Boba Fett=== [[File:Boba_on_Kamino.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Garr's best friend, Boba Fett]] Fellow ten-year-old [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]] came aboard the ship at [[Raxus Prime/Legends|Raxus Prime]] after he was saved from the [[Conquest of Raxus Prime]] by a group of [[Clone trooper/Legends|clone troopers]] and the [[Bothan/Legends|Bothan]] [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Glynn-Beti]]. Fett was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] boy who had never had a friend before, but Garr quickly became his first and best friend. After several [[Standard day/Legends|days]] of friendship, Fett asked if Garr was a boy or a girl, and Garr explained the difference in the Excargans and other [[Species/Legends|species]]. Garr found it frustrating that other children would not simply treat each other as people instead of girls and boys. Fett said that the other kids were stupid and should just treat Garr as a friend like Fett did. Garr and Fett loved to explore the ''Candaserri'' despite the prohibition against orphans roaming the ship unattended. Garr did not share Fett's interest in space, but both children were interested in starships and observed them being armed and serviced by [[Droid/Legends|droids]] in the rear docking bay. Even though Fett had been concealing his past, he unthinkingly revealed that [[Jango Fett/Legends|his father]] had taught him how to [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] a ship, something Garr's mother would have had a fit over. In all of their explorations of the ship, Garr and Fett had been unable to access the [[Bridge (starship)/Legends|bridge]]. The pair's mutual curiosity almost resulted in being left floating in space when they went on a spacewalk to look into the bridge from the outside. ===Orphans on Bespin=== [[File:Aurrasing_detail.png|thumb|left|200px|Garr's life was endangered by the bounty hunter Aurra Sing.]] Upon arriving at Bespin, Fett tried to escape from the Jedi at the orphanage; he hated the entire [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] due to recently witnessing [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Mace Windu/Legends|Mace Windu]] kill his father, Jango Fett. Garr realized Boba had accidentally left his flight bag behind and caught up with him to return it. While Garr tried to persuade Fett to come back to the orphanage, the friends were interrupted by the [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Aurra Sing/Legends|Aurra Sing]]. Sing grabbed Fett and Garr began kicking her to get her to release him. In response, Sing dangled Garr over a [[Kilometer/Legends|kilometers]]-long drop, but pulled the child back after Fett called Garr his friend. Despite what he had just said, Fett then acted cold and repeated to Garr that he had no room for friends, trying to motivate Garr to survive by leaving him behind. Garr still would not flee until Sing's hand went to her [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]]. However, Garr had not abandoned Fett and told the Jedi what was happening with Sing. Garr joined Glynn-Beti and her [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]], [[Ulu Ulix]], in a [[Cloud car/Legends|cloud car]] to pursue Sing as she flew ''[[Slave I/Legends|Slave I]]'', the ship she had stolen from Fett. Sing was prepared to fire upon the cloud car until Fett flew in between to prevent her from doing so, even though it almost resulted in Fett losing his life. Although Garr seeking help from the Jedi seemed to Fett like a personal betrayal, the boy recognized that Garr had just intended to help him. ==Personality and traits== Compared to Boba Fett at the same age, Garr's complexion was darker than Fett's [[Color/Legends|tan-colored]] skin{{Film|II}} and Garr's [[Hair/Legends|hair]] was shorter. Garr had brown [[Eye/Legends|eyes]] that Fett thought had a merry look. Raised to respect the Jedi, Garr wished to be [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] and train as one of them. Despite their friendship, Garr only knew Boba Fett by the alias "Teff" that Fett used to avoid being identified by the Jedi. In addition to keeping his own name a secret, Fett concealed or lied to Garr about other personal details, such as his own origin as a [[Cloning/Legends|clone]] of his father when Garr felt the clone troopers were creepy. ==Behind the scenes== [[File:BobaFett_2_HC.jpg|thumb|right|160px|The hardcover edition of ''Boba Fett: Crossfire'']] Garr appeared in the young readers novel ''[[Boba Fett: Crossfire]]'', written by [[Terry Bisson]] and first published by [[Scholastic]] in [[2002]]. The book was the second in the [[Star Wars: Boba Fett (novel series)|''Star Wars: Boba Fett'']] series for young readers. Garr received an entry in the [[2008]] reference book set ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''. The character's entry refers to Garr with he/him pronouns{{CSWECite|I|325|Garr}} despite ''Boba Fett: Crossfire'' never using any pronouns for Garr. Due to other instances of ''The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia'' using he/him for characters never previously referred to with those pronouns, this article has treated the entry as in error. ==Appearances== *''[[Boba Fett: Crossfire]]'' {{1st}} ==Sources== *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' *''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |de=Garr }} [[Category:Excargans]] [[Category:Genderless individuals]] [[Category:Individuals from Excarga]]
